vuln_q_0096_0002.title: CWE-327,328 - Insecure hashing
vuln_q_0096_0002.description: A hashing algorithm is weak if it is easy to determine the original input from the hash or to find another input that yields the same hash. Weak hashing algorithms can lead to security vulnerabilities.
vuln_q_0096_0002.recommendation:
The hashing algorithm you are using is unsecure and might lead to cryptographic vulnerabilities. [Learn more](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hash_function_security_summary). Use a more secure hashing algorithm when creating message digests using hashlib. For example, use SHA224, SHA256, SHA384, or SHA512. 

[Learn more](https://docs.python.org/3/library/hashlib.html#hash-algorithms)

vuln_q_0081_0004.title: CWE-798 - Hardcoded credentials

vuln_q_0081_0004.recommendation: We detected credentials hardcoded in your code, which might allow unauthorized users to access your account. We recommend you to store your credentials outside of the code in a configuration file, a database, or a management service for secrets and retrieve from it. For more information, see [CWE-259](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html) and [CWE-798](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/798.html).

vuln_q_0081_0004.title: CWE-322 - Using AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy

vuln_q_0039_0003.title: CWE-200 - Insecure Socket Bind
vuln_q_0039_0003.description: Binding the socket with an empty IP address will allow it to accept connections from any IPv4 address provided, thus can introduce security risks.
vuln_q_0039_0003.recommendation:
Binding a socket with an empty IP address causes the bound address to default to 0.0.0.0. This might bind a socket to all interfaces, which opens the socket to traffic from any IPv4 address and creates security risks.

vuln_q_0025_0002.title: CWE-295 - Improper certificate validation
vuln_q_0025_0002.description: Lack of validation or insufficient validation of a security certificate can lead to host impersonation and sensitive data leaks.
vuln_q_0025_0002.recommendation:
When `verify` is set to `False`, requests will accept any TLS certificate presented by the server. Requests also ignore hostname mismatches or expired certificates, which make your application vulnerable to man-in-the-middle (MitM) attacks. Setting `verify` to `False` might be useful during local development or testing.

[Learn more](https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/advanced/#ssl-cert-verification) and[Learn more](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/295.html)

vuln_q_0025_0002.title: CWE-798 - Hardcoded credentials
vuln_q_0025_0002.description: Access credentials, such as passwords and access keys, should not be hardcoded in source code. Hardcoding credentials may cause leaks even after removing them. This is because version control systems might retain older versions of the code. Credentials should be stored securely and obtained from the runtime environment.
vuln_q_0025_0002.recommendation: We detected credentials hardcoded in your code, which might allow unauthorized users to access your account. We recommend you to store your credentials outside of the code in a configuration file, a database, or a management service for secrets and retrieve from it. For more information, see [CWE-259](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html) and [CWE-798](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/798.html).
